words.definition: Made up of the same kind of things.

words.definition: A person who helps others and cares about their well-being.

words.definition: Something that is imagined or not real yet.

words.definition: Someone who opposes traditional beliefs or practices.

words.definition: A behavior or way of thinking that is characteristic of a person.

words.definition: Something that brings shame or disgrace.

words.definition: Something that is not allowed or illegal.

words.definition: To light up or make something clear.

words.definition: Very famous and respected for past achievements.

words.definition: Having no stain or blemish; spotless, undefiled, clear, clean, pure.

words.definition: Something that is about to happen very soon.

words.definition: The state of being insusceptible to something; notably:

words.definition: To make something weaker or less effective.

words.definition: Treating everyone equally and fairly.

words.definition: To hinder, impede, or prevent.

words.definition: Perfect, without faults, flaws or errors

words.definition: A hindrance; that which impedes or obstructs progress.

words.definition: (grammar) The grammatical mood expressing an order (see jussive). In English, the imperative form of a verb is the same as that of the bare infinitive.
words.sentence: The verbs in sentences like "Do it!" and "Say what you like!" are in the imperative.

words.definition: Unaffected or unable to be affected by something.

words.definition: Something understood without being said directly.

words.definition: Not showing due respect; impertinent; bold-faced.

words.definition: Not intentional; not on purpose; not conscious.

words.definition: Something that gives off light when heated.

words.definition: Without pause or stop; not ending, especially to the point of annoyance.

words.definition: A physical tilt or bend.

words.definition: Two things that do not work well together.

words.definition: Not leading to a clear result or decision.

words.definition: Not easily seen or noticed.

words.definition: A small increase or addition.

words.definition: To accuse or bring criminal charges against.

words.definition: A formal charge of a crime.

words.definition: People or things that come from a specific place.

words.definition: Feeling angry about something unfair.

words.definition: Without care or making distinctions, thoughtless.

words.definition: One who is not eligible.

words.definition: Not skilled or clumsy at something.

words.definition: Unfairness or lack of justice.

words.definition: The tendency to stay still or keep moving.

words.definition: Without fault or weakness; incapable of error or fallacy.

words.definition: Any undesirable substance or group of cells that has made its way into part of the body.

words.definition: A non-zero quantity whose magnitude is smaller than any positive number (by definition it is not a real number).

words.definition: Displaying genius or brilliance; tending to invent.

words.definition: To try to please someone to gain their favor.

words.definition: Something that is a natural part of something else.

words.definition: To hold back or stop something from happening.

words.definition: Something you are born with or naturally have.

words.definition: Harmless or not likely to offend.

words.definition: Producing harm in a stealthy, often gradual, manner.

words.definition: The condition of being insolvent; the state or condition of a person who is insolvent; the condition of one who is unable to pay his debts as they fall due, or in the usual course of trade and business.

words.definition: To incite; to bring about by urging or encouraging

words.definition: Very hard to deal with or endure.

words.definition: A person who fights against the local government.

words.definition: Something you cannot touch or see, like feelings.

words.definition: A number, the limit of the sums computed in a process in which the domain of a function is divided into small subsets and a possibly nominal value of the function on each subset is multiplied by the measure of that subset, all these products then being summed.

words.definition: To speak for someone else to help them.

words.definition: An intermittent fever or disease.

words.definition: To introduce (something) between other things; especially to insert (possibly spurious) words into a text.
words.sentence: in verse 74, the second line is clearly interpolated

words.definition: To mix two things irregularly, placing things of one kind among things of other:

words.definition: Having a great deal of fine detail or complexity.

words.definition: Every time; always, without change.

words.definition: Lacking enthusiasm, energy, or strength; drooping or flagging from weakness, fatigue, or lack of energy

words.definition: Giving off light or glowing brightly.

words.definition: Strictly thermodynamic entropy. A measure of the amount of energy in a physical system that cannot be used to do work.
words.sentence: Ludwig Boltzmann defined entropy as being directly proportional to the natural logarithm of the number of microstates yielding an equivalent thermodynamic macrostate (with the eponymous constant of proportionality). Assuming (by the fundamental postulate of statistical mechanics), that all microstates are equally probable, this means, on the one hand, that macrostates with higher entropy are more probable, and on the other hand, that for such macrostates, the quantity of information required to describe a particular one of its microstates will be higher. That is, the Shannon entropy of a macrostate would be directly proportional to the logarithm of the number of equivalent microstates (making it up). In other words, thermodynamic and informational entropies are rather compatible, which shouldn't be surprising since Claude Shannon derived the notation 'H' for information entropy from Boltzmann's H-theorem.
